scott-reed.outreach_subject_override: World Action Models, and where the representation lives
scott-reed.outreach_body_override: Hi Scott — I partner with out-of-distribution people solving truly hard problems in AI, and I saw you're building something new. World Action Models stuck with me — the policy emerging from predicting how the world evolves, rather than trained separately like VLAs or GATO. I think that's the right frame. Where my head goes next is the representation: DreamZero and Cosmos predict in video space, but the JEPA bet is to predict in latent space instead — throw away the pixel detail that doesn't matter and model the abstract dynamics. I'm genuinely curious whether you see video as the representation that works, or a stepping stone toward predicting in representation space. (WAM almost feels like your 2016 generative work and the GATO thesis finally fused.) I write $500K–$1M at inception, but mostly I'd love to hear how you're thinking about it. — Vansh
